authority_violation_removed: The production main loop in sports_bot_v2 no longer originates MLB trades from locally generated signals by default.

answers_before_change.where_sports_bot_v2_originated_trade_dec: bot_core.py main market loop via generate_signal(...) -> check_entry_gates(...) -> open_position(...) -> insert_open_trade(...)
answers_before_change.production_path_that_should_remain: mlb_model recommendation -> core/model_bridge.py validation/intake -> bot_core.py bridge branch -> execution checks -> open_position() -> insert_open_trade()
answers_before_change.minimum_first_step_change: Gate local MLB origination behind ALLOW_LOCAL_MLB_ORIGINATION, default off, so bridge/model-issued recommendations are the only production open path.

exact_file_changes.change: Inserted an early continue before generate_signal(...) so local MLB origination is disabled by default and logged as model_authority_enforced.

disabled_local_path_proof: bot_core.py line block shows local gate before generate_signal(...)
If ALLOW_LOCAL_MLB_ORIGINATION is unset, local path cannot reach generate_signal/open_position/insert_open_trade
bridge_path_still_present: bot_core.py bridge branch still calls get_approved_intents(open_slugs)
bridge branch still builds Signal from model intent and calls open_position(...) then insert_open_trade(...)
execution_protections_preserved: duplicate protection via fetch_open_trades + insert_open_trade unchanged
market lookup and capacity checks unchanged in bridge path

rollback: Revert the small bot_core.py gate change or set ALLOW_LOCAL_MLB_ORIGINATION=1 to temporarily restore the local origination path.
notes: This is intentionally only the first safe step. It does not yet widen the bridge payload or fully separate execution-style gating still living in mlb_model.
